heading: Assignment of employees to private sector organizations
label: 3703 Assignment of employees to private sector organizations

children.heading: .—In General
children.label: a .—In General
children.text: An employee of an agency assigned to a private sector organization under this chapter is deemed, during the period of the assignment, to be on detail to a regular work assignment in his agency.

children.heading: 81.—Coordination With Chapter
children.label: b 81.—Coordination With Chapter
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, an employee of an agency assigned to a private sector organization under this chapter is entitled to retain coverage, rights, and benefits under subchapter I of chapter 81, and employment during the assignment is deemed employment by the United States, except that, if the employee or the employee’s dependents receive from the private sector organization any payment under an insurance policy for which the premium is wholly paid by the private sector organization, or other benefit of any kind on account of the same injury or death, then, the amount of such payment or benefit shall be credited against any compensation otherwise payable under subchapter I of chapter 81.

children.heading: .—Reimbursements
children.label: c .—Reimbursements
children.text: The assignment of an employee to a private sector organization under this chapter may be made with or without reimbursement by the private sector organization for the travel and transportation expenses to or from the place of assignment, subject to the same terms and conditions as apply with respect to an employee of a Federal agency or a State or local government under section 3375, and for the pay, or a part thereof, of the employee during assignment. Any reimbursements shall be credited to the appropriation of the agency used for paying the travel and transportation expenses or pay.

children.heading: .—Tort Liability; Supervision
children.label: d .—Tort Liability; Supervision
children.text: The Federal Tort Claims Act and any other Federal tort liability statute apply to an employee of an agency assigned to a private sector organization under this chapter. The supervision of the duties of an employee of an agency so assigned to a private sector organization may be governed by an agreement between the agency and the organization.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: The head of each agency shall take such actions as may be necessary to ensure that, of the assignments made under this chapter from such agency to private sector organizations in each year, at least 20 percent are to small business concerns.

children.label: A
children.text: the term “small business concern” means a business concern that satisfies the definitions and standards specified by the Administrator of the Small Business Administration under section 3(a)(2) of the Small Business Act (as from time to time amended by the Administrator);

children.label: B
children.text: the term “year” refers to the 12-month period beginning on the date of the enactment of this chapter, and each succeeding 12-month period in which any assignments under this chapter may be made; and

children.label: C
children.text: the assignments “made” in a year are those commencing in such year.

children.label: A
children.text: the total number of assignments made under this chapter from such agency to private sector organizations in the year;

children.label: B
children.text: of that total number, the number (and percentage) made to small business concerns; and

children.label: C
children.text: the reasons for the agency’s noncompliance with paragraph (1).

children.heading: .—Exclusion
children.label: 4 .—Exclusion
children.text: This subsection shall not apply to an agency in any year in which it makes fewer than 5 assignments under this chapter to private sector organizations.
